core_mechanism: coarse-graining of a large population of locally-interacting discrete-state units to derive emergent macroscopic collective order, with order-disorder (consensus/ordering) transitions arising purely from the local interaction/update rules
brief_reason: The abstract frames the work as studying 'collective phenomena emerging from the interactions of individuals as elementary units' and connecting them to statistical physics, i.e. the statistical-mechanics machinery of aggregating many locally-coupled units into emergent macroscopic order.
